attempts.strategy: Verbatim probe-confirmed body. Two-line tactic block: haveI extracts the class field via named-argument syntax (F := F) (𝒰 := 𝒰), then transport along (compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton. The compIso is taken as an explicit argument, not a class field — decouples Čech-side combinatorial vanishing (iter-048) from the substantive comparison theorem (iter-049+). Landed in same Edit as Target 1.
attempts.code_tried: theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)} {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰] (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ), Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) := by haveI := Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover.subsingleton_cechCohomology (F := F) (𝒰 := 𝒰) n hn; exact (compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton
attempts.goal_before: Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
attempts.insight: Explicit argument over class field for data: when a chain needs both a Prop-flavoured carrier (combinatorial vanishing) and a data-flavoured comparison (LinearEquiv), they cannot share a Prop-valued class. Keep the carrier as a class and pass the data as an explicit argument.

next_steps: Iter-049 supplies the comparison theorem that iter-048's consumer accepts via the explicit compIso argument. No further work on this consumer expected.
